framing: Freelance sports data analyst applying to head of analytics at MLS club

jd.title: Head of Football Analytics
jd.style_note: Vague/culture-first: passion for the game, light on technical specifics, relationships-focused

requirements.text: You live and breathe football — you watch matches not just for enjoyment but to understand tactical patterns and player tendencies
5+ years working with football/soccer data in a professional capacity (club, agency, media, or data provider)
You can build models and visualisations that help coaches and scouts make better decisions
You're comfortable presenting insights to football people, not just data people
Bonus: experience with tracking data (Second Spectrum, SkillCorner, or Metrica Sports)
Bonus: experience working inside a professional football club

responsibilities: Build and lead the analytics department for Inter Miami's first team and academy
Develop player recruitment models and opposition scouting reports using event and tracking data
Collaborate with coaching staff to deliver match preparation and post-match analysis
Manage relationships with data providers (Stats Perform/Opta, Second Spectrum)
Present analytical findings to sporting director, head coach, and ownership group

expected_output.summary: Freelance football data analyst with 6 years of experience building analytics tools and player evaluation models for professional clubs and agencies. Clients include 3 MLS clubs, 2 Championship (EFL) clubs, and 1 football agency. Built xG model used by 4 clubs for player recruitment. Published football analytics newsletter with 8,000 subscribers. Proficient in Python (pandas, scikit-learn), R (ggplot2), and Tableau. Former semi-professional footballer (USL League Two). No experience in full-time club analytics role.

experience.title: Freelance Football Data Analyst
bullets: Built custom expected goals (xG) and expected threat (xT) models for 4 professional clubs using StatsBomb Open Data and Opta event data
Delivered player recruitment shortlists for 3 MLS clubs using data-driven filtering and similarity models (Python, scikit-learn)
Created opposition scouting report templates in Tableau used by 2 EFL Championship coaching staffs
Built automated match report pipeline (Python) processing Opta event data into coaching-friendly PDF reports
Published weekly 'The xG Files' newsletter analysing MLS tactical trends — 8,000 subscribers

experience.title: Junior Data Analyst — Football
bullets: Coded live match events for MLS and USL Championship matches using Opta's proprietary coding platform
Performed data quality assurance on coded match event data (passes, shots, tackles) across 200+ matches
Supported client-facing analytics team with custom data extracts and visualisations for MLS club clients
Learned football analytics domain: expected goals, passing networks, pressing intensity, possession value models

education: BSBA Sport Business Management — University of Central Florida (2014-2018)

jd_requirements: football soccer data 5+ years professional club agency media data provider
presenting insights football people not data people
tracking data Second Spectrum SkillCorner Metrica Sports
deliberate_flaws: No tracking data experience (Second Spectrum, SkillCorner, Metrica Sports) — only event data (Opta/StatsBomb)
Never worked inside a professional football club full-time (freelancer, always external)
No experience building or leading an analytics department (solo freelancer)
No Spanish language skills (Inter Miami has significant Spanish-speaking staff)
No experience managing data provider relationships from the club side (was the provider at Opta)
No video analysis platform experience (Hudl Sportscode, Wyscout, InStat) — only data-driven, not video-driven
Freelancer — no experience with club politics, dressing room dynamics, or daily coaching staff interaction
Sports Management degree, not data science or statistics — technical skills are self-taught
MLS and EFL Championship experience — no top-5 European league analytics experience

provenance.notes: Synthetic persona and JD. JD style modeled from real Greenhouse/Lever postings. Expected output and cloud_skills are AI-generated. 4/8 scorecard checks (jd_coverage, no_fabrication, metrics_preserved, no_fabricated_skills) evaluate AI vs AI reference. 4/8 checks (factual_preservation, word_count, ats_structure, action_verbs) are structurally valid regardless.
